mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on equities and supports multiple expansion, so when policy is already near zero the S&P 500 should have positive 20-day forward returns. The signal fails if rates are rising or if the market has already priced the easing path.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% near the effective lower bound
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with FEDFUNDS at or below 0.25%, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions, raises equity discount rates, and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect should disappear when policy is already near zero.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with stable or falling PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration, which weakens expected household income and corporate sales, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when unemployment rises faster than payrolls are added.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 20-day forward return is not negative in months where UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth is below its 6-month average, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in RSAFS relative to recent months signals weakening consumer demand and lower near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should pressure the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days. The effect is falsified when retail sales are stable or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if months with a >1.0% decline in RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average return difference versus non-triggered months is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with rising HOUST indicates broad real activity and supply-side momentum, which should support earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if industrial production improves while housing collapses.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-HOUST improvement regime does not outperform the unconditional 60-day SPX return by at least 1 percentage point, or if the rolling 5-year hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in GDPC1 indicates slower aggregate demand and lower expected corporate cash flows, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when GDP growth is decelerating from an already weak level.
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend GDPC1 quarters are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal’s rolling 4-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, the labor market is deteriorating fast enough to impair consumption and margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This is an interaction rule: either series alone is weaker than the combined deterioration signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth < 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the interaction adds no predictive power beyond PAYEMS or UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while RSAFS also weakens, the combination indicates synchronized demand cooling in housing and consumer spending, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-conditional because it matters most after a late-cycle slowdown has already started.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-RSAFS weakness regime does not outperform the unconditional negative-return rate by at least 10 percentage points, or if the effect vanishes outside late-cycle periods rather than concentrating there.
